hooks.command: cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty' 2>/dev/null); echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'push.*--force|push.*-f' && { echo 'WARNING: Force push detected — this can destroy remote history' >&2; exit 2; }; exit 0

fp=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' 2>/dev/null); echo "$fp" | grep -qE '\.(env|env\..*)$' && ! echo "$fp" | grep -q '.env.example' && { echo 'BLOCKED: .env files contain secrets' >&2; exit 2; }; exit 0

cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty' 2>/dev/null); echo "$cmd" | grep -qE 'npm publish' && jq -n --arg ctx 'Published to npm. Remember to create a GitHub release with gh release create v<version>' '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"PostToolUse",additionalContext:$ctx}}'; exit 0
fp=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' 2>/dev/null); ext="${fp##*.}"; ([ "$ext" = "ts" ] || [ "$ext" = "tsx" ]) && npx prettier --write "$fp" 2>/dev/null; exit 0

fp=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty' 2>/dev/null); echo "$fp" | grep -q TASKS.md || exit 0; section=$(sed -n '/^## Current/,/^## /p' TASKS.md 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$section" ] && exit 0; unchecked=$(echo "$section" | grep -cF '- [ ]' || true); checked=$(echo "$section" | grep -cF '- [x]' || true); [ "$unchecked" -eq 0 ] && [ "$checked" -gt 0 ] && echo 'Sprint complete — all current tasks done. Consider a quick quality check before committing: scan for dead code, debug artifacts, TODO hacks, and convention violations. Run tests if available. Skip if trivial.'; exit 0
